description_long: This is a life-sized Holstein cow with a robust, elongated body and a prominent, slightly angular back. Its coat features a striking black and white piebald pattern, with large, irregular patches of black on a predominantly white background. The head is broad with a white blaze running down the face, flanked by two short, upward-pointing horns and large, alert ears. The legs are sturdy and proportionate, with white lower limbs and black upper sections. The udder is visible beneath the belly, indicating a female dairy cow. The tail hangs straight down, and the overall posture suggests a calm, standing animal suitable for rural or urban park settings.
description: A black and white Holstein cow with a distinctive piebald coat, short horns, and a visible udder, standing in a natural pose.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad face, white blaze, both horns, and symmetrical ears.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated body, prominent black and white patches, and visible udder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the straight back, tail hanging down, and the symmetry of the hind legs.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the body profile, distinct coat pattern, and the udder partially visible.
attribute_ear_shape: The ears are large, oval-shaped, and project outward from the sides of the head, with a slight upward tilt and rounded tips.
attribute_pattern_distinctiveness: The coat pattern is highly distinctive, with bold, sharply defined black patches contrasting against a clean white background, creating a visually striking and easily recognizable appearance.
